channel.title: Green Curve Insights
channel.description: Daily ESG and climate compliance intelligence for Indian businesses.

item.title: Climate Adaptation Startups 2026: What the Next Wave of Climate Tech Means for Indian Capital Allocation, BRSR Disclosure, and Supply Chain Resilience
item.description: GreenBiz's Climate Tech Startups to Watch 2026 pitch competition — focused explicitly on climate adaptation rather than mitigation — signals a structural pivot in global climate finance toward resilience, physical risk management, and adaptive infrastructure. For Indian CFOs and CSOs, this is not a

item.title: 99 Sustainability Certifications for 2026: What India's CSOs, CFOs, and ESG Teams Must Know Before They Invest in Credentials
item.description: Trellis (GreenBiz) has published its 2026 list of 99 sustainability certifications across 10 subcategories — the most comprehensive industry reference of its kind — reflecting an accelerating professionalisation of the ESG function globally. For Indian listed companies navigating SEBI's BRSR Core ma

item.title: UBS Elevates Sustainability and Climate Risk to a Dedicated C-Suite Function: What This Signals for Indian Borrowers, Exporters, and ESG-Linked Capital
item.description: UBS has appointed Alexander Seidler as Head of Sustainability and Climate Risk, consolidating the bank's SCR framework under a single accountable leader with explicit authority over client onboarding, transaction approvals, supplier assessments, and internal climate risk disclosures. Reporting by ES

item.title: Rio Tinto's $1.5B Low-Carbon Aluminium Commissioning: What AP60 and ELYSIS Mean for Indian Aluminium Importers, Auto OEMs, and BRSR Scope 3 Disclosers
item.description: Rio Tinto has begun commissioning a $1.5 billion AP60 aluminium smelter expansion at its Complexe Arvida facility in Quebec, producing metal at just 1.6 tCO2e per tonne — one-seventh of the global industry average of 10.9 tCO2e/t and half the emissions of the adjacent legacy smelter it is replacing.

item.title: Gigascale Capital's $250M Climate Tech Fund: What Early-Stage Physical Economy Investing Signals for India's Clean Energy Capital Stack
item.description: Gigascale Capital, founded by former Meta CTO Mike Schroepfer, has closed its first institutional fund at $250 million, targeting early-stage companies rebuilding the physical economy — clean energy, grid infrastructure, advanced manufacturing, and physical AI — with a clear thesis that cost competi

item.title: Rabobank's New CSO Appointment: What Leadership Continuity at a Top Agri-Finance Bank Signals for Indian Food & Agri ESG Borrowers
item.description: Rabobank — the world's leading agri-food bank with an active lending and investment book across Indian food processing, dairy, horticulture, and agribusiness — has appointed Dries Lagerberg as its new Chief Sustainability Officer, succeeding Aafke Keizer. Lagerberg's background as Head of Group Sust

item.title: EU's Anti-Greenwashing Directive Enforcement Begins: What India's Exporters, Listed Companies, and ESG Communicators Must Recalibrate Now
item.description: The European Commission has opened formal infringement procedures against 20 of 27 EU member states — including France, Germany's neighbours, Spain, and the Netherlands — for failing to transpose the Directive on Empowering Consumers for the Green Transition (ECGT) into national law by the March 27,

item.title: TD Bank's 10-Year CDR Portfolio Deal with Climeworks: What India's Financial Sector and Listed Companies Must Read Into the Carbon Removal Signal
item.description: TD Bank's 10-year, multi-technology carbon dioxide removal (CDR) agreement with Climeworks — spanning enhanced rock weathering, biochar, BECCS, and direct air capture — signals that Tier-1 global financial institutions are now institutionalising high-durability carbon removal as a structured, long-t

item.title: UK's 87% Emissions Cut by 2040: What India's Exporters, Investors, and Climate Policymakers Must Read Between the Lines
item.description: The UK government's proposed Seventh Carbon Budget — capping economy-wide GHG emissions at 535 MtCO2e for 2038–2042 and targeting an 87% reduction from 1990 levels by 2040 — is not a distant policy signal. For Indian exporters with UK supply chain exposure, Indian companies seeking London-listed cap

item.title: SLR's Acquisition of Anchor Environmental Signals a Global Arms Race in Natural Capital Advisory — Here's What Indian Boards Must Read Into It
item.description: SLR Consulting's acquisition of Cape Town-based Anchor Environmental Consultants — reported by ESG Today (https://www.esgtoday.com/slr-acquires-natural-capital-advisory-firm-anchor-environmental-consultants/) — is not merely an M&A footnote. It is a leading indicator that global ESG consulting infra

item.title: CPCB Tightens the Screws: What the Notice to E-Waste Recyclers Means for EPR Compliance
item.description: The Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) has issued a notice directing registered e-waste recyclers to strictly comply with the E-Waste (Management) Rules, 2022, signaling heightened scrutiny of the EPR (Extended Producer Responsibility) credit ecosystem. The move is a clear warning shot: recycler

item.title: CPCB's New EPR Certificate Framework for E-Waste: What Producers Need to Know About Metal-Based Targets
item.description: The Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) has released a framework that fundamentally changes how Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) certificates are generated under the E-Waste (Management) Rules, 2022 — moving from a unit/weight-based system to one anchored in actual metal recovery (Gold, Cop

item.title: CPCB Notice: Interim Arrangement for E-Waste EPR Credit Generation and Transfer by Recyclers
item.description: The Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) has issued an interim arrangement governing how registered E-Waste Recyclers generate and transfer EPR end-product credits (certificates) on the EPR Portal. This is a transitional measure under the E-Waste (Management) Rules, 2022, designed to keep the EPR

item.title: CPCB Interim Arrangement for Tyre Waste EPR Credits: What Recyclers and Producers Must Know
item.description: The Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) has issued an interim arrangement governing how registered tyre waste recyclers generate and transfer Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) end product credits — known as EPR certificates — on the centralised online portal. The move is designed to bridge o

item.title: Food Systems Under the ESG Lens: What India's Agri-Business Boards Must Act On Before BRSR Core and TNFD Disclosures Converge
item.description: Food and agriculture sits at the exact intersection of nature risk, climate transition, and supply chain disclosure — three regulatory currents converging simultaneously on Indian listed companies. GreenBiz's sustained coverage of this sector (trellis.net/topic/food-agriculture/) reflects a global e

item.title: Candour as Capital: Why Honest Sustainability Reporting Drives Shareholder Value — and What Indian CFOs Must Learn Before BRSR Core Enforcement Arrives
item.description: New research published by GreenBiz (trellis.net) confirms what sophisticated ESG investors have long suspected: companies that disclose sustainability shortcomings with specificity and accountability generate measurable share price outperformance, while glossy, kitchen-sink sustainability reports ac

item.title: SBTi's 2026–2030 Strategic Reset: What India's BSE 500 Companies, SBTi Commiters, and ESG Investors Must Recalibrate Now
item.description: The Science Based Targets initiative (SBTi) has published a landmark 35-page Strategic Plan for 2026–2030 that fundamentally repositions the organisation from a target-setting body to a 'transformation partner' — shifting toward sector-specific guidance, softening enforcement when companies miss tar

item.title: Decarbonisation at an Inflection Point: What Indian Boards Must Read Into the Global Momentum Shift
item.description: Global decarbonisation discourse is accelerating beyond voluntary pledges into mandatory measurement, verified target-setting, and capital allocation consequences — and Indian listed companies, particularly those in carbon-intensive and export-linked sectors, are now squarely in the crosshairs of th

item.title: IFS Zero AI Emissions Platform: What Asset-Intensive Indian Industrials Must Understand About the GHG Data Infrastructure Race
item.description: IFS — a global industrial software provider — has launched IFS Zero, an agentic AI-powered emissions measurement and reporting platform targeting asset-intensive industries such as manufacturing, energy, and infrastructure. The platform automates Scope 1, 2, and 3 GHG data collection, validation, an

item.title: California SB 253's Audit-Grade Carbon Data Mandate: Why Indian CFOs and CSOs Must Act Now
item.description: California's SB 253 — the Climate Corporate Data Accountability Act — mandates that companies with over $1 billion in annual revenue doing business in California disclose Scope 1, 2, and 3 emissions with independent third-party assurance, with Scope 1 and 2 reporting commencing August 2026 and Scope
